## Findings

### F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-001 — vol3 windows.info on /tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw reports Is6… *(confidence: HIGH)*

vol3 windows.info on /tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw reports Is64Bit True, NtMajorVersion 10, NtMinorVersion 0, Major/Minor 15.19041, NtProductType NtProductWinNt, NtSystemRoot C:\WINDOWS, and SystemTime 2020-11-16 02:32:38+00:00.. The memory image is from a 64-bit Microsoft Windows 10 workstation (kernel build 19041, i.e. version 2004/20H1), and the system clock at acquisition read 2020-11-16 02:32:38 UTC. This establishes the host OS and acquisition time baseline for all subsequent volatility analysis of this image..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw#windows.info kernel structures; build 15.19041` — tool `volatility3 windows.info`, command `vol -f /tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw windows.info`
- **Cited evidence:** `Is64Bit	True`, `NtMajorVersion	10`, `NtMinorVersion	0`, `Major/Minor	15.19041`, `NtProductType	NtProductWinNt`, `NtSystemRoot	C:\WINDOWS`, `SystemTime	2020-11-16 02:32:38+00:00` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 aa0e99d61146dc83…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-001`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-001` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `e85d2b806bf5922f…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-002 — vol3 windows.netscan shows process svchost.exe with PID 1248 ow… *(confidence: HIGH)*

vol3 windows.netscan shows process svchost.exe with PID 1248 owning TCP sockets in the LISTENING state on local address 0.0.0.0 port 3389 (TCPv4) and :: port 3389 (TCPv6), created 2020-11-11 08:13:16 UTC.. The Windows Remote Desktop Protocol service (Terminal Services, which runs inside an svchost.exe instance) is bound and listening on port 3389 across all network interfaces (0.0.0.0 / ::), meaning the host accepts inbound RDP connections from any network, including external networks. This exposes a remote-access service on the workstation..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw#PID 1248; TCP 0.0.0.0:3389 LISTENING; socket offset 0xb78cfadb94f0` — tool `volatility3 windows.netscan`, command `vol -f /tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw windows.netscan`
- **Cited evidence:** `0.0.0.0	3389	0.0.0.0	0	LISTENING	1248	svchost.exe`, `::	3389	::	0	LISTENING	1248	svchost.exe`, `2020-11-11 08:13:16.000000 UTC` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 4a02f60c9ce8a134…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-002`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-002` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `2501f3fda015830f…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-003 — vol3 windows.netscan shows 118 inbound TCPv4 connection records… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

vol3 windows.netscan shows 118 inbound TCPv4 connection records to local address 192.168.1.5 port 3389, owned by svchost.exe PID 1248, with foreign (source) addresses 81.30.144.115, 213.202.233.104, 201.193.188.114 and 81.19.209.101. States across these records are 113 CLOSED, 4 ESTABLISHED and 1 SYN_RCVD, and the Created timestamps span 2020-11-16 02:30:05 to 2020-11-16 02:36:24 UTC. Four records are ESTABLISHED: 213.202.233.104:45753, 81.30.144.115:51048, 81.30.144.115:5067, and 213.202.233.104:40876.. Within an approximately six-minute window the workstation (LAN address 192.168.1.5) received a high volume of inbound RDP connections to its port-3389 Terminal Services listener from a small set of non-RFC1918 (external/public) source IP addresses, dominated by 81.30.144.115 and 213.202.233.104. The large number of rapidly cycled CLOSED connections from these two source IPs is characteristic of automated RDP brute-force / password-spray activity against the exposed Remote Desktop service, and the 4 ESTABLISHED records show that TCP-level RDP sessions from those external IPs were live at the time of capture. This is external remote-access activity against the host and a plausible intrusion vector. NOTE: netscan establishes the TCP sessions and their endpoints only; it does not by itself prove that RDP authentication succeeded..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw#Local 192.168.1.5:3389, PID 1248; 118 records; window 2020-11-16 02:30:05-02:36:24 UTC` — tool `volatility3 windows.netscan`, command `vol -f /tmp/rocbamem/Rocba-Memory.raw windows.netscan`
- **Cited evidence:** `192.168.1.5	3389	213.202.233.104	45753	ESTABLISHED	1248	svchost.exe`, `192.168.1.5	3389	81.30.144.115	51048	ESTABLISHED	1248	svchost.exe`, `192.168.1.5	3389	81.30.144.115	5067	ESTABLISHED	1248	svchost.exe`, `192.168.1.5	3389	213.202.233.104	40876	ESTABLISHED	1248	svchost.exe`, `192.168.1.5	3389	81.19.209.101	50424	SYN_RCVD	1248	svchost.exe`, `192.168.1.5	3389	201.193.188.114	63385	CLOSED	1248	svchost.exe`, `2020-11-16 02:30:05.000000 UTC`, `2020-11-16 02:36:24.000000 UTC` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 05da6f12765bc641…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-003`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-003` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `6261559cdbcf1f1f…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

## Confidence & limitations

- Confidence is stated per finding (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / SPECULATIVE) and reflects what a single artifact
  can bear; nothing here is asserted beyond the cited evidence.
- "Observation" is the literal tool output; "interpretation" is the analyst's inference. The Council's
  Citation, Tool-semantics, Contradiction, Inference, and Scope seats independently checked the leap between them; Synthesis aggregated the verdicts.
- Every finding traces to its tool execution (`csift trace F-analyst-ROCBA-MEM-001`); concrete-command receipts can additionally be re-executed against evidence with `csift trace --rerun …`. Council Receipts are hash-chained.
- This narrative is generated from the audit substrate, not hand-written — it cannot contain a claim that
  is not a verified node in the graph.
